title: 11 Herbal Creams For Palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal creams and teas can be a great natural way to relieve palpitations. Palpitations are feelings of irregular heartbeats, which can be caused by stress, anxiety, or other health issues. Herbal teas, in particular, can help calm the nervous system and reduce palpitations. One reason why herbal teas are effective is that they contain compounds that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.  For example, Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a well-known herb for its calming properties. It contains valerenic acid, which can help slow down the heart rate and promote a good night's sleep. This can be especially beneficial for people who experience palpitations due to stress or anxiety. Another herb, Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, can also help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Its active compounds, flavonoids and alkaloids, can interact with neurotransmitters to calm the nervous system.  Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm, is another herb that can help alleviate palpitations. It contains rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce stress and anxiety by interacting with neurotransmitters. Lemon balm tea can be especially soothing, as it can help calm the mind and body. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can experience the benefits of reduced palpitations and improved overall well-being. This can lead to a more balanced and peaceful life, with reduced stress and anxiety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, isovalerenic acid, and valepotriates as its active constituents. These compounds have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate palpitations. Valerenic acid, in particular, has been shown to interact with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related heart rate increases. The calming properties of Valeriana officinalis can also help regulate heart rhythm and blood pressure, contributing to a more stable heart rate. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, Valeriana officinalis creams can be an effective natural remedy for palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis root, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grind the dried Valeriana officinalis root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Valeriana officinalis root powder to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool, and then pour it into a container. Allow it to solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams contains flavonoids and alkaloids as active constituents. Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that contribute to palpitations. Alkaloids, particularly harman and harmine, have a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and regulate heart rate. These compounds can also help reduce sympathetic nervous system activity, which is often overactive in individuals experiencing palpitations. By reducing stress and anxiety, Passiflora incarnata creams can help alleviate palpitations and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat until melted. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains essential compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and geranial, which have been identified in the plant's oil. These bioactive constituents have been found to possess anti-anxiety and anti-stress properties, helping to calm the nervous system. Melissa officinalis creams are also rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and promote relaxation. The plant's oil has been traditionally used to reduce palpitations and anxiety, and its calming properties can help alleviate symptoms of nervousness. By applying Melissa officinalis creams topically, the body can absorb the beneficial compounds, which can help to reduce palpitations and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Melissa officinalis oil, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Melissa officinalis oil and shea butter to the melted mixture. Stir until well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Melissa officinalis cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains the bioactive constituents linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to slow down heart rate and reduce palpitations by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. The antispasmodic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to regulate heart rhythm and alleviate palpitations. Additionally, the sedative properties of these constituents help to reduce stress and promote a sense of calm, further contributing to a reduction in palpitations. By reducing stress and anxiety, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to alleviate palpitations and promote a more stable heart rhythm.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil, and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Add lavender and peppermint essential oils and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and harden. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the risk of palpitations caused by blood pressure fluctuations. Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to palpitations. The vasodilating properties of gingerol and shogaol help to lower blood pressure and improve heart function, making it easier for the heart to pump blood. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams may help to reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations by promoting cardiovascular health.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated. Remove from heat.
Add Zingiber officinale powder and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use for palpitations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams contains a variety of bioactive constituents, including avenanthramides, avenacosides, and avenasterols. These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Avenanthramides, in particular, have been shown to have a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce palpitations. Avenacosides have also been found to have a positive impact on the cardiovascular system, helping to regulate heart rate and blood pressure. The combination of these properties in Avena sativa creams can help to alleviate palpitations by promoting a sense of calm and regulating the body's physiological responses.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup Avena sativa extract, 1/2 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons lavender oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add Avena sativa extract to the melted oil and beeswax. Stir well to combine.
Remove from heat and stir in vitamin E oil and lavender oil. Let cool until the mixture thickens.
Pour the mixture into a container and let cool completely. Once set, your Avena sativa cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis creams contains a key active constituent called baicalein, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce stress and anxiety, two major causes of palpitations. Another important constituent, baicalin, has a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing heart rate. The calming properties of Scutellaria baicalensis creams also help to regulate heart rhythm and improve blood flow, further alleviating palpitation symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Scutellaria baicalensis creams can help to alleviate the uncomfortable symptoms of palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Scutellaria baicalensis herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of Scutellaria baicalensis root powder, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan over low heat until it's warm.
In a separate bowl, mix the Scutellaria baicalensis root powder with the coconut oil and beeswax until well combined.
Slowly add the warm water mixture to the powder mixture, stirring constantly until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove from heat, add vitamin E oil, and pour into a container. Let cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains bioactive constituents like hyperforin and hypericin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety that can cause palpitations. Hyperforin also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate heart rate and blood pressure. Additionally, hypericum perforatum has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations. By reducing stress and anxiety and regulating heart rate and blood pressure, hypericum perforatum creams may be beneficial in managing palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds, until it's warm and melted.
Add 1/4 cup of the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another bowl, discarding the solids.
Store the Hypericum perforatum cream in a cool, dark place in an airtight container. Apply 2-3 times a day to the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have a calming effect on the heart and blood vessels, reducing their reactivity and promoting a more stable heart rhythm. The terpenoids, including ginkgolides A and B, also help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, further reducing palpitations. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and promoting a stable heart rhythm, Ginkgo biloba creams can help alleviate palpitations.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until melted.
Add the beeswax to the melted oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool, and solidify. Store the Ginkgo biloba cream in an airtight container in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm the nervous system. The flavonoids in the cream also have antioxidant effects, reducing oxidative stress that may contribute to palpitations. Glycyrrhizin, on the other hand, has a direct impact on the heart by regulating the rhythm and reducing the frequency of abnormal heartbeats. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams, the heart rate can be stabilized, and palpitations may be reduced.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in olive oil, glycerin, and Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or tin and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, use a spoon or spatula to scoop out a small amount of the cream and apply it to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams contains triterpenoid saponins, including asiatic and madecassic acid, which are known to have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to reduce stress and anxiety, leading to a decrease in palpitations. The anti-inflammatory properties of centella asiatica's glycosides, such as madecassoside, also contribute to its ability to regulate heart rate and blood pressure. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of the extract's flavonoids, like quercetin, help to protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage. By addressing the underlying causes of palpitations, centella asiatica creams can provide relief and promote a more stable heart rhythm.
remedies.image_alt: Centella asiatica herbal creams for palpitation
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Centella asiatica extract, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until melted and smooth.
Add Centella asiatica extract and stir well. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add vitamin E oil and aloe vera gel to the mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Centella asiatica cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help calm your nerves and reduce palpitations. Certain teas, like chamomile and lavender, have natural soothing properties that can ease anxiety and promote relaxation. When consumed regularly, these teas might help stabilize heart rhythms and minimize the occurrence of palpitations. Their calming effects can be beneficial for people experiencing stress-induced palpitations.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be calming for palpitations, consuming them daily may not be safe for everyone. Some teas, like licorice root, can interact with medications and worsen heart problems. Others, like foxglove, can be toxic in large amounts. It's essential to be aware of the potential risks and choose teas that are specifically labeled as safe for heart conditions.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with palpitations, but it depends on the type and amount used. Some herbal teas, like passionflower and lavender, may help calm the heart within 30 minutes to an hour. Others, like ginger and peppermint, might take a few hours to a full day to show results, as they help relax the body and reduce stress.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for palpitations can help calm your heart. It's best to sip it in the morning or early afternoon, before your heart rate picks up with stress and activity. This way, the soothing effects of the tea can stay with you throughout the day, helping to regulate your heartbeat.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for palpitations. Certain herbs like ginseng and hawthorn can increase heart rate and blood pressure, making palpitations worse. Other herbs like kava and valerian can slow heart rate, but in some cases, it can also cause drowsiness and interact with medications that already slow heart rate.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be careful with herbal teas. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can help calm palpitations, but others, like ginger and raspberry leaf, might cause issues. If you're experiencing palpitations, talk to your healthcare provider about the safest options. They can guide you on what teas are safe to consume during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for palpitations while breastfeeding are generally considered safe, but it's a good idea to check the ingredients first. Some herbs like chamomile, lavender, and peppermint are commonly used and thought to be harmless for both mom and baby. However, strong or excessive consumption of certain herbs, like sage or yarrow, might affect milk production or the baby's health.
title_2: Elderly and Palpitation: 11 Herbal Creams for a Healthy Heart
